translation: Tyrion: 塞、[p/][s/]塞莱斯特，[p/]冷静点——

text: Celeste: So you can take your attitude and leave.[p/] I don’t need your help.
translation: Celeste: 你可以就这么回去了。[p/]我不需要你的帮助。

text: Ruby: Fine,[p/] have it your way.
translation: Ruby: 好吧，[p/]随你的便。

text: Ruby: But when you’re staring down the gallows and begging me to appeal your conviction,[p/] I’ll be charging you triple my rate.
translation: Ruby: 但当你站在绞刑架前，求我为你上诉的时候，[p/]我可得收你三倍的费用。

text: Before I could understand what was going on,[p/] Ms. Tymora turned away and stormed out.
translation: 在我理解发生了什么事之前，[p/]泰摩拉女士已经转过身去，匆匆地离开了。

text: As she reached the door,[p/] she looked back at me expectantly.
translation: 她走到门口时，[p/]她回头看了我一眼，脸色有所期待。

text: I froze.[p/] I knew I wasn't in any position to handle a case like this.
translation: 我呆住了。[p/]我知道自己根本没有能力处理这样的案子。

text: ...That said,[p/] I also knew I couldn't leave her to face this alone.
translation: ……但反过来说，[p/]我也知道不能让塞莱斯特就这么独自面对这件事。

text: My mentor let out a quiet sigh before quietly exiting the dungeons.
translation: 我的导师轻叹一声，然后离开了地牢。

text: Celeste: Sorry...[p/] I didn’t mean to put you in the middle of that.
translation: Celeste: 对不起……[p/]我不是想要让你被夹在中间。

text: Tyrion: ...I can go talk to her.[p/] I'm sure she'll come back and take your case.
translation: Tyrion: ……我会和她谈一谈。[p/]我相信她会回来处理你的案子。

text: Celeste: No,[p/] I meant what I said.[p/] I don’t want her help.
translation: Celeste: 不，[p/]正如我所说的。[p/]我不想要她来处理。

text: Tyrion: Celeste![p/][s/] This is serious!
translation: Tyrion: 塞莱斯特！[p/][s/]这开不得玩笑！

text: Tyrion: Your conviction might as well be a foregone conclusion to the courts.[p/] Without Ms. Tymora,[p/] you’ll—
translation: Tyrion: 对法院来说，给你定罪几乎是板上钉钉的事。[p/]没有泰摩拉女士的话，[p/]你会——

text: Celeste: It'll be fine.
translation: Celeste: 那也没关系。

text: Celeste: I know I just pulled you into this without asking.
translation: Celeste: 我明白，是我擅自把你拉到这个处境的。

text: Celeste: If you want to walk away,[p/] I’ll understand.[p/] I’ll figure something out...
translation: Celeste: 如果你不愿意，[p/]我也理解的。[p/]我会自己找些办法……

text: She tugged a few times at the cell door separating us.[p/] I could have sworn I saw the metal bars bending.
translation: 她拉了拉隔开我们的牢房门。[p/]我确信，我亲眼看到几根金属条弯曲了。

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: I exhaled slowly.[p/] I knew I wasn’t ready for this.
translation: 我缓缓吐出一口气。[p/]我知道自己还没有准备好。

text: Tyrion: ...I can’t stand by and do nothing.
translation: Tyrion: ……但我不能就这么撒手不管。

text: Tyrion: I’ll take your case,[p/] if you’ll have me.
translation: Tyrion: 只要你愿意，[p/]我就接下这个案子。

text: Celeste: Really?
translation: Celeste: 真的吗？

text: Tyrion: ...But there’s one thing I need you to tell me.
translation: Tyrion: ……但有一件事需要你告诉我。

text: The Arcane Inquisition was called to the scene.
translation: 奥术裁判所被叫到了犯罪现场。

text: I had no idea how they even knew magic had been used,[p/] but their presence could only mean one thing.
translation: 我不知道他们怎么知道现场有使用过法术，[p/]但他们的现身本身就只意味着一件事情。

text: Tyrion: ...Are you a mage?
translation: Tyrion: ……你是法师吗？

text: Celeste: ...Y-[p/][s/]Yeah.[p/] I am.
translation: Celeste: ……是、[p/][s/]是的。[p/]我确实是。

text: When that villager confronted me before,[p/] he called her a bastard.[p/] It made a lot more sense now.
translation: 之前那个村民质问我的时候，[p/]他把她骂作“野种”。[p/]这就说得通了。

text: Tyrion: ...Do you have noble blood?
translation: Tyrion: ……你有贵族的血统吗？

text: Celeste: ...What does [i]that[/i] have to do with anything?
translation: Celeste: ……那跟[i]这个案子[/i]有什么关系？

text: Tyrion: The ability to use magic is hereditary.
translation: Tyrion: 使用法术的能力是遗传下来的。

text: Tyrion: Almost every mage descends from the nobility in some way.
translation: Tyrion: 几乎每个法师都是通过某些途径继承自贵族们。

text: Tyrion: If you have birth parents in the nobility,[p/] we might be able to use their influence to our advantage.
translation: Tyrion: 如果你的生身父母有贵族的话，[p/]我们就有可能用他们的影响力带来优势。

text: Celeste: I don't need anyone else's help.[p/] I’m a McCoy.[p/] That’s all I need to know about myself.
translation: Celeste: 我不需要其他人的帮助。[p/]我的姓氏是麦考伊。[p/]那就是我需要知道的全部内容。

text: It seemed best not to push that matter further then.
translation: 看来不应该在这个方向上推进话题了。

text: Tyrion: ...Even so,[p/] that’s probably why they arrested you.
translation: Tyrion: ……即使如此，[p/]这大概仍然是他们要逮捕你的原因。

text: Celeste: What?
translation: Celeste: 什么意思？

text: Tyrion: Ms. Tymora mentioned it earlier.[p/] A town of farmers like Kornhaven wouldn’t have any other registered mages.
translation: Tyrion: 泰摩拉女士之前提到过。[p/]科尔哈文这样的农村不会有其他在册的法师。

text: Tyrion: It's a good thing you registered with the kingdom.[p/] It would have just added one extra charge if you hadn't.
translation: Tyrion: 你有向王国注册是件好事，[p/]否则你还要受到其他指控。

text: Tyrion: But they found traces of magic at the scene of the crime.[p/] They have no choice but to suspect you.
translation: Tyrion: 但他们在犯罪现场发现了魔法痕迹。[p/]而他们没有其他的选项，唯独怀疑了你。

text: Celeste: ...Are you serious?
translation: Celeste: ……你是认真的？

text: Tyrion: I’ll be honest.[p/] I’m not sure how much help I’ll really be.
translation: Tyrion: 我是认真的。[p/]我不清楚自己能帮上多大的忙。

text: Tyrion: Ms. Tymora said it herself.[p/] I've never defended a client before.
translation: Tyrion: 就像泰摩拉女士说的，[p/]我之前从没有为其他人辩护过。

text: Celeste: ...Is everything really going to be okay?
translation: Celeste: ……一切真的会顺利发展吗？

text: ...Why would I say something like that?
translation: ……我为什么要这样说？

text: A defense attorney should be putting their clients at ease.
translation: 辩护律师应该让自己的客户感到安心。

text: Tyrion: B-[p/][s/]But no matter what,[p/] I'll get you an acquittal.
translation: Tyrion: 不、[p/][s/]不论发生什么，[p/]我会让你无罪释放的。

text: Tyrion: It doesn't matter who stands in my way—[p/]man,[p/] king,[p/] or god.
translation: Tyrion: 不论是谁挡在我的面前——人、[p/]王、[p/]亦或是神。

text: Celeste: ...What?
translation: Celeste: .……啊？

text: Tyrion: S-[p/][s/]Sorry.[p/][s/] I used to say that a lot as a kid...[p/][s/] Um—
translation: Tyrion: 不、p/][s/]不好意思。[p/][s/]我小时候经常这么说……[p/][s/]那个——

text: Celeste: [i]*giggle*[/i] No,[p/] it's okay.
translation: Celeste: [i]*暗笑*[/i]没事，[p/]挺好的。

text: At least it made her laugh.
translation: 至少让她笑出来了。

text: Tyrion: ...A-[p/][s/]Anyways,[p/] take some time to rest up.[p/] The trial will begin in a few hours.
translation: Tyrion: .……总、[p/][s/]总之，[p/]抓紧时间休息一下。[p/]庭审几小时后就开始了。

text: I couldn’t imagine what she was going through.
translation: 我无法想象她正在经历什么。

text: She’d not only lost her father,[p/] but she was also being framed for his murder.
translation: 她不仅失去了自己的父亲，[p/]她还被栽赃为杀害父亲的凶手。

text: :But this was exactly why I became an attorney:[p/] to defend people exactly like her.
translation: :但这正是我成为辩护律师的原因：[p/]保护像她这样的人。

text: Stories like hers were far too common,[p/] but this time,[p/] I had the power to change it.
translation: 像她这样的悲剧太过常见了，[p/]但这一次，[p/]我有改变结局的力量。

text: She was putting her trust in me.[p/] I would make sure she wouldn't regret that.
translation: 她毫无保留地信任我。[p/]我必须让她不会因此后悔。

text: It still hadn’t sunk in yet.[p/] I was standing in a real courtroom.
translation: 我还没缓过神来，[p/]就已经站在了真正的法庭之上。

text: I'd only ever acted as Ms. Tymora's assistant.[p/] Now I was front and center at the defense table...
translation: 我只担任过泰摩拉女士的助手。[p/]现在是我自己站在辩护席的正中央……

text: Celeste: ...Are you okay?
translation: Celeste: ……你还好吗？

text: Tyrion: Y-[p/][s/]Yes.[p/][s/] Of course.
translation: Tyrion: 没、[p/][s/]没问题。[p/][s/]当然了。

text: Maybe if I kept saying that,[p/] it would end up being true...
translation: 或许只要我一直这么念下去，[p/]就真的会镇定下来……

text: AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: That woman from the dungeons was also here.[p/] She must’ve been the prosecutor for Celeste’s trial.
translation: 在地牢里碰到的那个女人也在这里。[p/]她肯定就是负责塞莱斯特这个案子的检察官。

text: No wonder she was able to order around the Commander like that.
translation: 难怪她能那么使唤那个指挥官。

text: I could feel her piercing glare burrowing into me.
translation: 我能感到她锐利的目光像针一样刺进我的身体。

text: Why was she looking at me like that?[p/] Was she trying to intimidate me?
translation: 她为什么要那样看着我？[p/]是不是想威慑我？

text: ...Because it was definitely working!
translation: ……因为还真的很有用！

text: Judge: Court is now in session for the trial of Celeste McCoy.
translation: Judge: 法庭现在开庭，审理塞莱斯特·麦考伊案。

text: AriaC: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.
translation: AriaC: 检方已准备就绪，法官阁下。

text: Tyrion: ...O-[p/][s/]Oh,[p/] uh.
translation: Tyrion: 噢、[p/][s/]噢，[p/]呃……

text: Tyrion: T-[p/][s/]The defense is also ready, Your Honor.
translation: Tyrion: 辩、[p/][s/]辩方也准备好了，法官阁下。

text: Judge: ...Very well.[p/] Prosecutor Steelwind,[p/] could you please list the charges?
translation: JUDGE: ……很好。[p/]检察官斯蒂尔文德，[p/]能请你列出指控吗？

text: AriaC: The defendant is Celeste McCoy:[p/] a mercenary by trade and a registered mage.
translation: Aria: 被告名叫塞莱斯特·麦考伊，[p/]工作是雇佣兵，[p/]并且是注册在案的法师。

text: AriaC: She’s charged with second-degree murder and negligent misuse of the Arcane Arts.
translation: AriaC: 她被指控二级谋杀罪以及过失使用奥术。

text: AriaC: The crime occurred within the McCoy Tavern:[p/] a local inn that was shut down years ago.
translation: AriaC: 案件发生在麦考伊酒馆：[p/]这是一个当地已停业多年的酒馆。

text: AriaC: Those who lived nearby reported hearing a loud argument coming from within the building around 1:00 AM.
translation: AriaC: 根据邻近居民报告，凌晨1:00左右听到酒馆内传来一阵大声的争吵。

text: AriaC: At 1:08 AM,[p/] a carriage driver entered the tavern to investigate the disturbance.
translation: AriaC: 凌晨1:08，[p/]一个马车夫走进酒馆来调查问题。

text: AriaC: It was there that he found the victim,[p/] the defendant’s sword impaled within his body.
translation: AriaC: 他在此时发现了被害者，[p/]被告的剑刺在他的尸体里。

text: AriaC: The Inquisition has provided an autopsy report and documentation for the sword.
translation: AriaC: 裁判所已正式提交尸检报告和那把剑的档案。

text: Judge: What was the defendant’s relationship with the victim?[p/] I see that they share the same last name.
translation: Judge: 被告与受害人是什么关系？[p/]我看到他们同姓。

text: AriaC: The defendant is the victim’s daughter,[p/] but not by blood.
translation: AriaC: 被告系被害人的女儿，[p/]但没有血缘关系。

text: AriaC: She was adopted into the family when she was an infant.[p/] Her birth parents are currently unknown.
translation: AriaC: 她在襁褓时便被麦考伊家收养，[p/]她的亲生父母目前未知。

text: Judge: I see...[p/] What an unfortunate turn of events...
translation: Judge: 原来如此，[p/]最后变成这样可真是不幸……

text: AriaC: To begin,[p/] the prosecution would like to call Commander Orym White to the stand.
translation: AriaC: 首先，[p/]检方希望传唤指挥官奥蒙·怀特出庭作证。

text: AriaC: Witness,[p/] please state your name and occupation for the court.
translation: AriaC: 证人，[p/]请向法庭陈述你的姓名与职业。

text: Orym: ...
translation: Orym: ……

text: AriaC: Witness.[p/] Name and occupation.
translation: AriaC: 证人。[p/]姓名与职业。

text: Orym: ...Name’s Orym White.[p/] I’m the commander of the Arcane Inquisition.
translation: Orym: ……我叫奥蒙·怀特，[p/]是奥术裁判所的指挥官。

text: AriaC: Commander,[p/] please testify about the details of the Inquisition's investigation.
translation: AriaC: 指挥官，[p/]请就裁判所的调查详情作证。

text: Orym: Yeah,[p/] yeah...
translation: Orym: 好、[p/]好吧……

text: Orym: We got a call late at night.[p/] Someone found a dead body inside the McCoy Tavern.
translation: Orym: 我们深夜时分接到报警。[p/]有人在麦考伊酒馆内发现了一具尸体。

text: Orym: When we arrived on the scene,[p/] we found Flinhart McCoy dead on the floor.[p/] The defendant’s sword was still in his chest.
translation: Orym: 当我们赶到现场时，[p/]我们发现倒在地板上的弗林哈特·麦考伊已经死亡。[p/]被告的剑仍插在受害者的身上。

text: Orym: We have a bunch of witnesses who heard the victim and defendant in a loud argument before the body was called in.
translation: Orym: 尸体被发现之前，有几个证人听到了受害者与被告的激烈争吵。

text: Orym: That argument must've turned ugly,[p/] because the defendant killed the old man using her magic sword.
translation: Orym: 他们的争吵十分激烈，以至于被告用她的魔法剑杀害了那个老家伙。

text: This was going to be difficult.[p/] The defendant's sword was the murder weapon.
translation: 这还挺麻烦的。[p/]被告持有的剑就是凶器。

text: It was possible that the murderer used it to frame her.[p/] That must have meant that the crime was premeditated.
translation: 当然很可能是真凶用它栽赃塞莱斯特。[p/]那一定意味着犯罪现场遭到过伪装。

text: Judge: Mr. Cuthbert, you may begin your cross-examination.
translation: Judge: 卡斯伯特先生，[p/]你可以开始交叉询问了。

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: Tyrion: Uh...
translation: Tyrion: 呃……

text: Judge: ...Mr. Cuthbert?
translation: Judge: ……卡斯伯特先生？

text: ...Where was I supposed to start again?
translation: ……我该从哪里开始来着？

text: I needed to prove that Celeste was innocent,[p/] but they found her sword in the body.
translation: 我需要证明塞莱斯特无罪。[p/]但他们发现她的剑刺在尸体上。

text: Celeste: Tyrion...?[p/] This is the part where you say something, right?
translation: Celeste: 提利昂……？[p/]这里应该是你该说些什么，对吧？

text: The longer I stood there,[p/] the more I noticed everyone staring at me...
translation: 我呆站得越久，[p/]我注意到越多的人开始盯着我……

text: I could feel the case cracking.[p/] Each moment of silence was just another blow to my credibility.
translation: 我能感到这个案子的根基在动摇。[p/]但沉默的每一秒都是对我名誉的一次打击。

text: AriaC: [i]*sigh*[/i]
translation: AriaC: [i]*叹气*[/i]

text: AriaC: I wanted to face Ruby Tymora today,[p/] not her bumbling sidekick.
translation: AriaC: 我想与鲁比·泰摩拉对决，[p/]而不是她笨手笨脚的小跟班。

text: Tyrion: ...!
translation: Tyrion: ……！

text: AriaC: Your Honor.[p/] The defense clearly has nothing to say against the prosecution’s overwhelming evidence.
translation: AriaC: 法官阁下。[p/]很显然辩方对检方的充分立证没有任何异议。

text: Orym: Yep.[p/] This whole thing’s pretty open and shut.
translation: Orym: 就是。[p/]这案子再清楚不过。

text: Tyrion: N-[p/][s/]No![p/][s/] Wait!
translation: Tyrion: 不、[p/][s/]不对！[p/][s/]等一等！

text: Tyrion: I-[p/][s/]I—
translation: Tyrion: 我、[p/][s/]我……

text: Ruby: …
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Ruby: I-[p/][s/]I’m so sorry, Tyrion.
translation: Ruby: 我、[p/][s/]我很抱歉，提利昂。

text: Ruby: I didn't do enough for Frey...[p/] Now she's—
translation: Ruby: 我为芙雷做得还不够好……[p/]而她现在——

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Tyrion: M-[p/][s/]Ms. Tymora...[p/][s/] Does this mean they are going to execute her?!
translation: Tyrion: 泰、[p/][s/]泰摩拉女士……[p/][s/]这意味着他们要对她处以极刑吗？！

text: No... [p/]Not again...
translation: 不……[p/]不要又一次……

text: Was I seriously going to watch that happen again?!
translation: 我真的要眼睁睁看着那种事情再次发生吗？！

text: Celeste: Tyrion!
translation: Celeste: 提利昂！

text: Her palm cracked across my face.[p/] I stumbled back and almost fell over.
translation: 她一巴掌打在我脸上。[p/]我被打得退了一大步，差点摔倒在地。

text: She had every right to be angry,[p/] but that wasn't what I saw on her face.
translation: 她有权对我发火，[p/]但我从她脸上看到的不是这样。

text: Tyrion: I-[p/][s/]I’m sorry...! Celeste,[p/] I—
translation: Tyrion: 对、[p/][s/]对不起，塞莱斯特！[p/]我——

text: Celeste: Stop.[p/] Just listen to me.
translation: Celeste: 停。[p/]听我的。

text: Celeste: You trained for this, right?[p/] You [i]know[/i] what to do.
translation: Celeste: 你一直为此训练，对吧？[p/]你[i]知道[/i]该怎么做。

text: Tyrion: ...I’ve never done this before.
translation: Tyrion: ……但我从来没有做过。

text: She cursed under her breath.[p/] She searched her mind for an answer.
translation: 她低声咒骂了一句。[p/] 她努力思考着答案。

text: Celeste: ...Then just treat it like something you [i]have[/i] done before!
translation: Celeste: ……那就把它当成你[i]做过[/i]的某件事就好了！

text: Celeste: I don’t know—[p/] Um...
translation: Celeste: 我也不太清楚——[p/]嗯……

text: Celeste: You’re good at chess, right?
translation: Celeste: 你很会下棋，不是吗？

text: Celeste: So just treat this whole thing like you’re playing chess!
translation: Celeste: 那就把这一切都当成在下棋就好了！

text: Tyrion: ...What?
translation: Tyrion: ……怎么了？

text: Celeste: I don't know![p/][s/] Yesterday,[p/] you were calm and you were focused!
translation: Celeste: 我真搞不懂！[p/][s/]昨天，[p/]你那么冷静又专注！

text: Celeste: So just...[p/][s/] [i]channel[/i] that feeling!
translation: Celeste: 所以只要……[p/][s/][i]联结[/i]那种状态！

text: Celeste: Look at the...[p/] pieces on the board...[p/] or something like that?
translation: Celeste: 把这一切看成……[p/]棋盘上的棋子……[p/]就像那样的事情？

text: Celeste: I—
translation: Celeste: 我——

text: He made a lot of statements.
translation: 他刚作出了大量证词。

text: Every statement that he made,[p/] every word that came out of his mouth,[p/] they were all just pieces on the board.
translation: 他所说的每一句话，[p/]他嘴里蹦出的每一个字，[p/]都只是棋盘上的棋子。

text: A match wasn't won from the first move.[p/] It came from developing the pieces I had and taking every advantage that presented itself.
translation: 不走出第一步的话，永远赢不了比赛。[p/]只有不断挪动棋子，抓住每一个机会才有可能赢下来。

text: Tyrion: ...I apologize, Your Honor.[p/] The defense is ready to begin the cross-examination.
translation: Tyrion: ……向您致歉，法官阁下。[p/]辩方已准备好开始交叉询问。

text: Judge: ...Hmph...[p/] Seeing as this is your first trial, Mr. Cuthbert,[p/] I’ll overlook it this one time.
translation: Judge: .……哼嗯……[p/]卡斯伯特先生，看在这是你第一次上庭的份上，[p/]我就放过这一次。

text: AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: Celeste: There we go. [h="Thank the gods|that actually worked..." e="hf"/]
translation: Celeste: 那我们开始吧。[h="感谢上帝啊，|这还真的有用……" e="hf"/]

text: I needed to carefully examine every statement he made,[p/] line by line.
translation: 我得逐句仔细检查每条证词。

text: The key to every cross-examination was identifying which statement contradicted the evidence in my NOTES.
translation: 交叉询问的关键就是找出哪一条证言与我<size=1.5em>记录</size>里的证据相矛盾。

text: Once I did that,[p/][s/] I needed to PRESENT the contradicting evidence!
translation: 只要一发现这种情况，[p/][s/]只需<size=1.5em>出示</size>有矛盾的证物就行！

text: Orym: We got a call late at night.[p/] Someone found a dead body inside the McCoy Tavern.
translation: Orym: 我们深夜时分接到报警。[p/]有人在麦考伊酒馆内发现了一具尸体。

text: Tyrion: What time did you get this call?[p/] And who discovered the body?
translation: Tyrion: 你什么时候接到的报警？[p/]又是谁发现了尸体？

text: Orym: The call came in at 1:08 AM,[p/] and the witness wanted to remain incognito.
translation: Orym: 我们凌晨1:08接到传讯。[p/]而那位证人希望保持匿名。

text: It wasn’t unusual for a witness to request anonymity.[p/] It was well within their rights.
translation: 证人要求保持匿名并不奇怪。[p/]这确实是他们拥有的权利。

text: But it would’ve been a lot easier if I could cross-examine that witness directly.
translation: 但如果能直接对那个证人进行交叉询问就方便多了。

text: AriaC: What happened when you arrived, Commander?
translation: AriaC: 指挥官，当你到达现场的时候是什么情形？

text: Orym: When we arrived on the scene,[p/] we found Flinhart McCoy dead on the floor.[p/] The defendant’s sword was still in his chest.
translation: Orym: 当我们赶到现场时，[p/]我们发现倒在地板上的弗林哈特·麦考伊已经死亡。[p/]被告的剑仍插在受害者的身上。

text: Tyrion: How are you so sure that the sword was my client’s?
translation: Tyrion: 你为什么确定那把剑属于我的委托人？

text: Orym: Her sword is made out of some weird alloy.
translation: Orym: 她的剑使用了某种特别的合金。

text: Orym: I think it was forged out of the country or something.
translation: Orym: 我觉得是在国外铸造的。

text: Orym: It’d be hard to mistake something like that.
translation: Orym: 那把剑可不容易弄混。

text: AriaC: We also have several eyewitnesses who verified that the sword belonged to the defendant.
translation: AriaC: 我们也有几位目击证人能证明那把剑是被告的。

text: AriaC: She made no effort to hide it from the public. Quite the contrary, she was quite proud to show it off.
translation: AriaC: 她并没怎么在公共场合试图遮掩它。恰恰相反，她很骄傲地到处炫耀。

text: Tyrion: Even so,[p/] that doesn’t necessarily mean that she was the one who stabbed him.
translation: Tyrion: 即便如此，[p/]这并不一定意味着她就是刺杀受害者的那个人。

text: Orym: Yeah,[p/] well...
translation: Orym: 行，[p/]好吧……

text: Orym: We have a bunch of witnesses who heard the victim and defendant in a loud argument before the body was called in.
translation: Orym: 尸体被发现之前，有几个证人听到了受害者与被告的激烈争吵。

text: Tyrion: ...How did they know it was my client who was arguing with the victim?
translation: Tyrion: ……而且他们怎么知道受害者争吵的对象就是我的委托人呢？

text: Orym: You were there, weren’t you?[p/] Don’t tell me you didn’t hear them.
translation: Orym: 你当时不也在场吗？[p/]别告诉我你没听见。

text: Orym: Every witness we talked to knew it was them.
translation: Orym: 每个证人都说是他们在吵架。

text: I couldn’t really argue with him there.[p/] Celeste and Flinhart had very distinct voices, especially when they were raised.
translation: 我无法在这个问题上驳倒他。[p/]塞莱斯特和弗林哈特的声音非常独特，尤其是他们还提高了嗓门。

text: Orym: That argument must've turned ugly,[p/] because the defendant killed the old man using her magic sword.
translation: Orym: 他们的争吵十分激烈，以至于被告用她的魔法剑杀害了那个老家伙。

text: Tyrion: I was there when the argument took place.[p/] Flinhart was still alive when my client left the tavern.
translation: Tyrion: 争吵发生的时候我的确在场。[p/]但当我的委托人离开酒馆的时候，弗林哈特还活着。

text: AriaC: Perhaps,[p/] but how do you know that she didn’t return to murder her father afterwards?
translation: AriaC: 或许是这样，[p/]但你怎么知道她没有在之后返回来杀害自己的父亲？

text: Tyrion: ...Weren’t there plenty of other people around who could have committed the crime?
translation: Tyrion: ……当时现场附近有很多可以犯罪的人不是吗？

text: Orym: That tavern’s been closed for years.
translation: Orym: 那个酒馆都关门几年了。

text: Orym: The defendant,[p/] the victim,[p/] and Tymora were the only ones there before the body was discovered.
translation: Orym: 尸体被发现之前，就只有被告、[p/]受害者、[p/]还有泰摩拉在场。

text: Orym: Plus,[p/] the murder weapon was her sword.
translation: Orym: 再加上，[p/]凶器就是她的佩剑。

text: It was a given that Ms. Tymora didn’t do this.[p/] Another person must have shown up at the tavern after Celeste and I left.
translation: 很显然泰摩拉女士不是凶手。[p/]一定有另一个人在塞莱斯特和我离开后来到了酒馆。

text: Could it have been the witness who discovered the body?
translation: 那个人是否有可能就是发现尸体的匿名证人？

text: AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: Overflow
His testimony was straightforward,[p/] but that’s exactly what would help me.
translation: 他的证言很直白，[p/]正好帮了我大忙。

text: Celeste was innocent,[p/] so there had to be a flaw in his logic.
translation: 因为塞莱斯特是无罪的，[p/]所以他的逻辑中一定有疏漏。

text: I just needed to compare each statement to each piece of evidence in my NOTES.
translation: 我只需将他的证言与我<size=1.5em>笔记</size>中的证据一一比对。

text: Once I found the contradiction,[p/] I just needed to PRESENT that piece of evidence to the contradicting statement.
translation: 一旦我找到与证据相悖的发言，[p/]就只要对着那句证言<size=1.5em>出示</size> 相矛盾的证据。

text: Player: Objection
translation: Player: 异议

text: Tyrion: Your Honor![p/][s/] It's clear that the witness's testimony is contradictory!
translation: Tyrion: 法官阁下！[p/][s/]证人的证言明显存在矛盾！

text: Tyrion: And this piece of evidence proves it!
translation: Tyrion: 而这件证据证明了这点！

text: Judge: I fail to see how that proves anything, Mr. Cuthbert...
translation: Judge: 卡斯伯特先生……我没看出来这两者有什么关系。

text: Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/] I guess you're right.
translation: Tyrion: 噢、[p/][s/]哦……[p/]我想您说得对。

text: Tyrion: Commander White,[p/] are you sure that the victim was killed using the defendant’s sword?
translation: Tyrion: 怀特指挥官，[p/]你确定受害者是被被告的佩剑刺死的吗？

text: Orym: Yeah...[p/] It’s pretty obvious from the crime scene.
translation: Orym: 当然了……[p/]从犯罪现场的情况来看再明显不过。

text: Tyrion: I think I’d have to disagree with you on that.
translation: Tyrion: 我想我不太同意你的看法。

text: Tyrion: If your testimony is true,[p/] the defendant’s sword couldn’t have been the murder weapon.
translation: Tyrion: 如果你的证言准确无误，[p/]那被告的佩剑就绝无可能是凶器。

text: Orym: What are you talking about, kid?
translation: Orym: 小子，你在胡言乱语什么？

text: Orym: The sword was inside his body.
translation: Orym: 那把剑就插在被害者的身体里。

text: Tyrion: That may be,[p/] but take another look at the Autopsy Report.
translation: Tyrion: 或许如此，[p/]但再仔细看看尸检报告。

text: Tyrion: It says that the fatal wound contained traces of [b]transmutation[/b] magic.
translation: Tyrion: 上面写着致命伤口上探测出[b]形变术[/b]的魔法痕迹。

text: Tyrion: You even testified that the sword was magical.
translation: Tyrion: 你甚至在证言里提到那是把魔法剑。

text: Orym: So?[p/] What’s your point?
translation: Orym: 所以呢？[p/]你想说什么？

text: Tyrion: It’s simple.
translation: Tyrion: 很简单。

text: Tyrion: How could a non-magical blade leave traces of magic in the wound?
translation: Tyrion: 一把没有附魔的剑怎么在伤口上留下魔法痕迹?

text: Orym: ...What?
translation: Orym: .……嗯？

text: AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: Tyrion: My client’s sword may look unique,[p/][s/] but it’s entirely non-magical.
translation: Tyrion: 我委托人持有的这把剑可能造型独特，[p/][s/]但完全是没有魔力的。

text: Tyrion: It couldn’t have left those traces of [b]transmutation[/b] on the wound.
translation: Tyrion: 它无法在伤口上留下[b]形变术[/b]的痕迹。

text: Orym: That doesn’t make a lick of sense.
translation: Orym: 这一点道理都没有。

text: Orym: If the sword didn’t kill him,[p/] then why was it in the body?
translation: Orym: 如果凶器不是这把剑，[p/]那它为何插在尸体上？

text: Tyrion: There’s only one reasonable explanation.
translation: Tyrion: 那就只有一种可能的解释。

text: Tyrion: The wound was created magically,[p/][s/] and someone shoved the sword into the wound afterward.
translation: Tyrion: 伤口本身是法术造成的，[p/][s/]之后某人用那把剑插入了同一道伤口。

text: Orym: And?[p/] That girl's a mage.
translation: Orym: 然后呢？[p/]那女孩是法师。

text: Orym: She could’ve easily whacked that old man using magic.
translation: Orym: 她本就可以轻易用法术干掉那个老头。

text: Tyrion: Why would she murder the victim using a spell,[p/] only to then stab him with her sword?
translation: Tyrion: 她为什么要先用法术杀死被害人，[p/]然后又用自己的剑刺死他？

text: Tyrion: She would just be incriminating herself.
translation: Tyrion: 她那样做只会不打自招。

text: Orym: ...
translation: Orym: ……

text: Tyrion: If anything,[p/][s/] this series of events proves something else.
translation: Tyrion: 非要说有什么意义的话，[p/][s/]那就是这一系列情况证明了其他的可能。

text: Tyrion: Someone used magic to murder the victim,[p/][s/] then pushed my client’s sword into the wound to frame her!
translation: Tyrion: 那就是某人使用法术杀死了被害者，[p/][s/]然后用我委托人的剑刺进那道伤口来栽赃给她！

text: AriaC: Don’t get ahead of yourself, Mr. Cuthbert.
translation: AriaC: 别太着急下结论，卡斯伯特先生。

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: AriaC: Your Honor,[p/] the defense is suggesting that another person used the sword to frame the defendant.
translation: AriaC: 法官阁下，[p/]辩方假定另有其人利用那把剑栽赃给被告。

text: AriaC: But the defendant clearly cast a spell on the sword at the time of the murder.
translation: AriaC: 但被告显然是在刺杀被害者时对那把剑施放了法术。

text: Judge: She cast a spell?
translation: Judge: 她使用了法术？

text: AriaC: Commander,[p/] testify as to how magic was used during the murder.
translation: AriaC: 指挥官，[p/]就谋杀过程中法术怎么被使用进行作证。

text: Orym: What...?
translation: Orym: 什么……？

text: AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: Orym: Alright, alright...[p/] I see where you’re going with this.
translation: Orym: 好吧、好吧……[p/]我明白你的意思了。

text: Orym: The sword itself ain’t magical,[p/] but spells can still be cast on it.
translation: Orym: 那把剑本身不是魔法剑，[p/]但仍然可以对它施放魔法。

text: Orym: That defendant over there?[p/] Her Spell Compendium's got a spell called "[b]Mage Blade[/b]".
translation: Orym: 被告的[b]法术汇编[/b]证实，她会一种叫做“[b]法术剑刃[/b]”的法术。

text: Orym: She must've cast it on her sword before stabbing the old man.
translation: Orym: 她肯定在刺向那个老头之前对自己的剑施放了那个法术。

text: Orym: That spell is the reason why we found traces of magic on the body.
translation: Orym: 那个法术正是我们在尸体上发现魔法痕迹的原因。

text: A Spell Compendium listed every spell a mage knew how to cast.
translation: [b]法术汇编[/b]会逐条列出一个法师知道施放的每一条法术。

text: It was a fundamental piece of evidence that proved which spells a mage could and couldn’t use.
translation: 那是证明一个法师会或者不会施放某条法术最基本的证据。

text: Tyrion: Celeste,[p/] do you have your Spell Compendium with you?
translation: Tyrion: 塞莱斯特，[p/]你带着自己的法术汇编吗？

text: Celeste: N-[p/][s/]No...[p/] My dad was the last person who had it.
translation: Celeste: 没、[p/][s/]没有……[p/]我爸爸就是最后拿着它的那个人。

text: What...?[p/] Why would Flinhart have her Spell Compendium?
translation: 什么……？[p/] 为什么弗林哈特会拥有她的法术汇编？

text: Celeste: But he's right.[p/] I know how to cast [b]Mage Blade[/b].
translation: Celeste: 但他说得对。[p/]我确实会释放[b]法术剑刃[/b]。

text: Celeste: It’s actually the only spell I know.
translation: Celeste: 那其实是我唯一会用的法术。

text: AriaC: I would like to add that we found traces of [b]transmutation[/b] magic on the defendant’s sword as well.
translation: AriaC: 我补充一下，被告的剑上同样被探测出[b]形变术[/b]的痕迹。

text: AriaC: As I'm sure the court knows,[p/] magical traces stay on objects for forty-eight hours after a spell is cast on them.
translation: AriaC: 我想在场各位都清楚，[p/]魔法痕迹会在法术施放后于对象物体上残留48个小时。

text: Those forty-eight hours really tightened the timeline for cases like this.
translation: 这48小时确实大大缩短了此类案件的处理时间。

text: Because of that,[p/] crimes needed to be investigated before all the magical traces disappeared from the crime scene.
translation: 正因如此，[p/]在犯罪现场的魔法痕迹消失前，所有关于案件的调查必须完成。

text: ...It also gave the courts the perfect excuse to push these cases through the system as quickly as possible.
translation: ……而这就给了法庭一个完美的理由，让他们得以用尽可能快的速度推进庭审。

text: Judge: Mr. Cuthbert, you may begin your cross-examination.
translation: Judge: 卡斯伯特先生，[p/]你可以开始交叉询问了。

text: The commander’s testimony was a lot vaguer this time.[p/] He probably wasn’t expecting to give a second testimony.
translation: 指挥官这次的证词更加模糊了。[p/]他大概没有准备要第二次作证。

text: PRESSing his statements really helped in situations like this.
translation: <size=1.5em>追问</size>在这种情况下非常有效。

text: Doing that might reveal new pieces of evidence that I could use against him.
translation: 那样做很可能揭露一些我可以用以反对他的新证据。

text: Orym: The sword itself ain’t magical,[p/] but spells can still be cast on it.
translation: Orym: 那把剑本身不是魔法剑，[p/]但仍然可以对它施放魔法。

text: Tyrion: Are you accusing my client of casting a spell and channeling it through her sword?
translation: Tyrion: 你是要指控我的委托人对她有施放过，而且是用她的剑传递了法术？

text: AriaC: There are a vast number of spells that operate like that.
translation: AriaC: 许多法术都可以这么操作。

text: AriaC: Spells from the school of [b]transmutation[/b] are almost exclusively channeled through an object.
translation: AriaC: 而[b]形变术[/b]学派的法术更是几乎都由外物传递。

text: AriaC: Your client clearly learned a spell that would aid in her work as a mercenary.
translation: AriaC: 你委托人学习的这条法术显然对她雇佣兵的工作很有帮助。

text: AriaC: Speaking of which...
translation: AriaC: 说到这里……

text: Orym: That defendant over there?[p/] Her Spell Compendium's got a spell called "[b]Mage Blade[/b]".
translation: Orym: 被告的[b]法术汇编[/b]证实，她会一种叫做“[b]法术剑刃[/b]”的法术。

text: Tyrion: "[b]Mage Blade[/b]"?
translation: Tyrion: "[b]法术剑刃[/b]"?

text: Celeste: It’s a [b]transmutation[/b] spell that sharpens and empowers my sword.
translation: Celeste: 这种[b]形变术[/b]可以让我的剑变得更锋利，增强威力。

text: Tyrion: Wouldn’t it be excessive for the defendant to use a spell like that on the victim?
translation: Tyrion: 被告对受害者使用这样的咒语，是否太过分了？

text: Tyrion: He wasn’t wearing any armor or protection at the time of his death.
translation: Tyrion: 在他死前，他没有穿戴任何装甲或护具。

text: AriaC: The victim and the defendant were in an intense argument.
translation: AriaC: 受害者与被告当时发生了激烈的争吵。

text: AriaC: Perhaps she used the spell in a fit of rage.[p/] Perhaps she simply cast it out of habit.
translation: AriaC: 或许她是盛怒之下用出那道法术。[p/]也或许那只是她的习惯。

text: AriaC: What matters is that the sword undeniably has traces of [b]transmutation[/b] magic on it.
translation: AriaC: 重要的是那把剑上不可否认地附有[b]形变术[/b]的痕迹。

text: Orym: She must've cast it on her sword before stabbing the old man.
translation: Orym: 她肯定在刺向那个老头之前对自己的剑施放了那个法术。

text: Tyrion: Why would she go to the trouble of doing that?
translation: Tyrion: 她干嘛非得要多此一举呢？

text: Tyrion: Her sword alone would have been enough to murder a defenseless person.
translation: Tyrion: 她那把剑本身就足够杀死一个没有防护的人。

text: Orym: Mages are always looking for an excuse to throw their power around.
translation: Orym: 法师总在找借口肆意施展法术。

text: Orym: There’s no rhyme or reason to it.
translation: Orym: 这方面他们是不讲道理的。

text: Tyrion: Isn’t that just your own personal bias, Commander?
translation: Tyrion: 那难道不是指挥官你的个人偏见吗？

text: AriaC: We don’t need to understand why the defendant did what she did.
translation: AriaC: 我们不需要搞清楚被告为什么怎么做，而是她到底做了什么。

text: AriaC: The fact of the matter is that traces of magic were found on the body and the sword.
translation: AriaC: 事实就是魔法的痕迹同时附着在尸体与她那把剑上。

text: AriaC: Do you have any evidence of the contrary, Mr. Cuthbert?
translation: AriaC: 你有证据可以反驳这一点吗，卡斯伯特先生？

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: Orym: That spell is the reason why we found traces of magic on the body.
translation: Orym: 那个法术正是我们在尸体上发现魔法痕迹的原因。

text: Tyrion: About that...[p/] Spells only leave magical traces on the objects they target,[p/] and those traces are only detectable for forty-eight hours.
translation: Tyrion: 关于这点嘛……[p/]法术只会在施放目标上留下魔法痕迹，[p/]而这些痕迹仅能在施放后的48小时内被探测到。

text: Tyrion: If my client cast [b]Mage Blade[/b] like you said,[p/] there shouldn’t have been any magical traces on the wound.[p/] There would only be traces on the sword.
translation: Tyrion: 如果我的委托人确实是如你所说施放了[b]法术剑刃[/b]，[p/]那被害人的创口上就不该有任何魔法痕迹。[p/]那样的话痕迹只应该附在剑上。

text: Orym: We always find random magical traces at crime scenes.[p/] We can’t always explain each trace we detect.
translation: Orym: 我们经常会在犯罪现场发现一些没规律的魔法痕迹。[p/]我们本来也没法解释每一道痕迹的来源。

text: Tyrion: That sounds more like a failure on the Inquisition’s part than anything else.
translation: Tyrion: 听起来这只是你们裁判所的失职。

text: Tyrion: Can we really trust that your knights examined the crime scene properly?
translation: Tyrion: 我们真的能相信你们这些骑士有充分搜查现场的情况？

text: Orym: You don’t believe me?[p/] Take a look at this picture.
translation: Orym: 你不相信我是吧？[p/]那就看看这张照片。

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: I’d seen pictures of dead bodies while studying Ms. Tymora’s past cases,[p/] but there was something unnerving about seeing someone I’d met yesterday.
translation: 在学习泰摩拉女士过去经手的案件时，我已经看过很多尸体的照片了。[p/]然而看到一个昨天才认识的人出现在这类照片上还是令我不安。

text: I carefully inspected the photo,[p/] taking great care to keep it out of Celeste’s view.
translation: 我小心地检视照片，[p/]注意地将它保持在塞莱斯特的视线之外。

text: Orym: You can see it here plain as day.
translation: Orym: 照片上面看着再直白不过。

text: Orym: The sword has the highest concentration of [b]transmutation[/b] traces.
translation: Orym: 那把剑上的[b]形变术[/b]痕迹最为集中。

text: ...But why didn’t those traces fully cover the weapon?
translation: ……可为什么魔法痕迹没有覆盖整把剑？

text: Did the [b]transmutation[/b] spell damage the sword somehow?
translation: 难道是[b]形变术[/b]不知怎么对这把剑造成了损伤？

text: AriaC: ...Why was this picture not brought to my attention, Orym?
translation: AriaC: ……奥蒙，为什么这张照片没有提交给我？

text: Orym: What would be the point of that?
translation: Orym: 那有什么必要呢？

text: Orym: This entire case is open and shut.
translation: Orym: 这案子清晰明了。

text: AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: AriaC: ...!
translation: AriaC: ……！

text: The prosecutor seemed unnerved by something.
translation: 检察官看起来因为什么事情变得紧张。

text: Orym: You can see it right in that photo.[p/] She stabbed him right through the chest using [b]Mage Blade[/b].
translation: Orym: 你能在那张照片里看到。[p/]她用[b]法术剑刃[/b]刺穿了被害人的胸膛。

text: He was right about the photo.
translation: 他对照片的描述是正确的。

text: It clearly showed that he was stabbed from the front.
translation: 上面确实显示被害人是从前方被刺杀的。

text: ...So why did that bother me so much?
translation: ……所以我为什么这么在意这一点呢？

text: Overflow
His testimony was a little bit vague this time.
translation: 他这次的证词有一点模糊。

text: PRESSING his statements might prove useful in a situation like this.
translation: 在这种情况下，<size=1.5em>追问</size>证词或许会有用处。

text: That would let me ask questions about his testimony and discover more details about the case.
translation: 那可以让我对他的证词进一步问询，找出案件中更多的细节。

text: I might even be able to obtain new pieces of evidence to use in my cross-examination.
translation: 甚至可能找出一些可以在交叉询问有用的新证据。

text: Objection
translation: 异议

text: Tyrion: Your Honor![p/][s/] It's clear that the witness's testimony is contradictory!
translation: Tyrion: 法官阁下！[p/][s/]证人的证言明显存在矛盾！

text: Tyrion: And this piece of evidence proves it!
translation: Tyrion: 而这件证据证明了这点！

text: Judge: I fail to see how that proves anything, Mr. Cuthbert...
translation: Judge: 卡斯伯特先生……我没看出来这两者有什么关系。

text: Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/] I guess you're right.
translation: Tyrion: 噢、[p/][s/]哦……[p/]我想您说得对。

text: Tyrion: I’m sorry, Commander White,[p/] but you’ve shown us an even more glaring contradiction.
translation: Tyrion: 怀特指挥官，痕抱歉。[p/]但你为我们展露了一个更明显的矛盾。

text: Orym: Oh, yeah?
translation: Orym: 哦，真的吗？

text: Tyrion: In your testimony,[p/] you said that my client stabbed the victim "right through the chest".
translation: Tyrion: 你的证词提到，[p/]我的委托人“从正面”刺穿了被害人的胸膛。

text: Orym: You can see it right there in that picture.
translation: Orym: 你在照片里能很清楚的看到啊。

text: Orym: So, where’s your "contradiction"?
translation: Orym: 所以，你说的“矛盾”在哪儿呢？

text: Tyrion: That’s exactly the problem, Commander.
translation: Tyrion: 这正是问题所在，指挥官。

text: Tyrion: Your picture undeniably shows that my client's sword impaled the victim from the front,[p/] but if that’s really true—
translation: Tyrion: 你提交的照片显示，我委托人的佩剑从正面刺穿被害人，[p/]但如果确实如此——

text: Tyrion: —Why does the Autopsy say that he died from being stabbed in his back?!
translation: Tyrion: ——那为什么尸检报告显示他是死于来自后背的刺伤？

text: Orym: ...What?!
translation: Orym: ……什么？！

text: AriaC: Orym![p/][s/] You were supposed to submit [i]everything[/i] you had to me!
translation: AriaC: 奥蒙！[p/][s/]你应该把你知道的[i]一切内容[/i]提交给我！

text: AriaC: Why did you withhold this photo?!
translation: AriaC: 你为什么扣下了这张照片？！

text: Orym: T-[p/][s/]There was no point!
translation: Orym: 因、[p/][s/]因为这没什么意义啊！

text: Orym: She's a mage![p/][s/] It couldn’t have been anyone else!
translation: Orym: 她是个法师！[p/][s/]没可能是其他人作案了！

text: Murmurs rippled through the gallery.[p/] Even the judge shifted uncomfortably in his seat.
translation: 旁听席上泛起一阵窃窃私语。[p/]甚至法官也在座位上不安地挪动起来。

text: Our justice system may have been corrupt,[p/] but they’d never get away with such a blatant error.
translation: 我们的司法系统或许早已腐坏，[p/]但他们也难以无视如此明显的错误。

text: Judge: Order![p/][s/] Order in the court!
translation: Judge: 肃静！[p/][s/]注意法庭秩序！

text: Judge: Mr. Cuthbert, what is the meaning of this?!
translation: Judge: 卡斯伯特先生，这是怎么回事？！

text: Tyrion: Your Honor,[p/] this photo clearly shows my client’s sword impaling him from the front!
translation: Tyrion: 法官阁下，[p/]这张照片的确清楚地显示了我委托人的佩剑从前方刺穿了被害人！

text: Tyrion: Which means there's only one way for the autopsy to be true!
translation: Tyrion: 这意味着只剩下一种符合尸检报告的记录的可能性！

text: Tyrion: The sword would've needed to be inserted into the wound [i]after[/i] the victim had died!
translation: Tyrion: 这把剑必然是在受害者被杀死[i]之后[/i]才插入他的伤口！

text: Orym: T-[p/][s/]That’s crazy!
translation: Orym: 这、[p/][s/]这太荒谬了！

text: Tyrion: Then why don't you enlighten me, Commander White?
translation: Tyrion: 那您为什么不开导我们一下，还有什么可能呢，怀特指挥官？

text: Tyrion: Do you think my client impaled the victim hilt-first with her sword?!
translation: Tyrion: 你认为我的委托人是用剑柄刺穿了受害者吗？

text: Orym: I—
translation: Orym: 我——

text: Tyrion: Or do you think she stabbed him through the chest,[p/] removed it,[p/] and then placed her sword in the body to frame herself?!
translation: Tyrion: 或者你认为她是先从后方刺穿了他的胸膛，[p/]然后又拔出来，[p/]再然后又将她的剑插回去来栽赃给她自己吗？

text: Orym: Well,[p/] [i]that's[/i]—
translation: Orym: 好吧，[p/] [i]也可能[/i]——

text: Tyrion: No.[p/][s/] What happened here is obvious!
translation: Tyrion: 没有可能。[p/][s/]实际发生的情况已经很明显了！

text: Tyrion: The real murderer stuck the sword through the victim’s wound to implicate my client!
translation: Tyrion: 真正的凶手将剑刺入受害者的伤口，嫁祸给我的委托人！

text: Tyrion: But they made a careless mistake and stuck the sword through the opposite side!
translation: Tyrion: 但他犯了个粗心的错误，把剑插反了！

text: Celeste: You’re actually pretty good at this...!
translation: Celeste: 你真的很擅长这个哎……！

text: AriaC: It appears I underestimated you,[p/] but you’ve still gotten ahead of yourself.
translation: AriaC: 是我小看你了。[p/]但你还是太着急下结论。

text: Judge: I-[p/][s/]Is that so, Ms. Steelwind?
translation: Judge: 是、[p/][s/]是这样吗，斯蒂尔文德女士？

text: AriaC: ...Let me ask the defense one question, Mr. Cuthbert.
translation: AriaC: ……那我要问辩方一个问题，卡斯伯特先生。

text: AriaC: If your client didn’t kill Flinhart McCoy,[p/] then who did?
translation: AriaC: 如果杀害弗林哈特·麦考伊的人不是你的委托人，[p/]那谁是？

text: Tyrion: ...How should I know?
translation: Tyrion: ……我为什么会知道？

text: Tyrion: If I'm not mistaken,[p/] it's the Commander's job to figure that out.
translation: Tyrion: 如果我的推论没有问题，[p/]找出真凶是那边的指挥官的工作。

text: AriaC: Oh,[p/] I’m well aware of the Commander’s incompetence,[p/] but one fact still stands.
translation: AriaC: 噢，[p/]我当然已经注意到了指挥官的失职责任，[p/]但仍有一个事实不容辩驳。

text: AriaC: There were magical traces on the wound and the sword.
translation: AriaC: 魔法的痕迹同时附着在尸体与她那把剑上。

text: AriaC: Would you like me to tell you how many mages live in that small town?
translation: AriaC: 你能否告诉我在那座小村庄里有几个法师呢？

text: I didn't like where she was going with this.
translation: 我不希望她继续说下去。

text: AriaC: Only one.[p/] The defendant is the only registered mage who lives in Kornhaven.
translation: AriaC: 只有一名。[p/]被告是科尔哈文里唯一一个注册的法师。

text: Right...[p/] Kornhaven was a village of farmers.[p/][s/] Celeste was the only mage there!
translation: 是啊……[p/] 科尔哈文是个只有农民的村子……[p/][s/]塞莱斯特是那里唯一的法师！

text: AriaC: The defendant was also established to have been with the victim minutes before his time of death.
translation: AriaC: 经查证，被告在受害人死亡前几分钟曾与其在一起。

text: AriaC: In addition to that,[p/] I have several witnesses who heard them arguing.
translation: AriaC: 再补充一句，[p/]我们还有好几位目击者当时听到他们的争吵。

text: AriaC: You,[p/] on the other hand,[p/] can only implicate an unknown assailant.
translation: AriaC: 而你，[p/]与此同时，[p/]只能提出一个未知的袭击者。

text: AriaC: I doubt you could even tell me who they are.
translation: AriaC: 我怀疑你是否有能力告诉我那究竟会是谁。

text: Tyrion: ...Even so,[p/] it’s clear that the prosecution can’t reliably prove how the victim was murdered.
translation: Tyrion: ……即便如此，[p/]很显然检方并没能可靠地证明被害人究竟如何遭到杀害。

text: Judge: Hm...[p/] I can’t say that the prosecution’s evidence is irrefutable,[p/] but the evidence still leans heavily against the defense.
translation: Judge: 嗯……[p/]检方提供的证据确实不能说是无可辩驳的，[p/]但这些证据仍然对辩方极为不利。

text: Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: Judge: There are far too many unanswered questions for me to pass a verdict.
translation: Judge: 太多未解之谜萦绕此案，因此我无法在此下达判决。

text: Judge: We will give the Inquisition time to investigate the crime scene and clear these unknowns.
translation: JUDGE: 我们将给予裁判所一些时间来充分调查犯罪现场，解释这些未解之谜。

text: Judge: ...I suggest that they conduct their investigation more thoroughly this time.
translation: Judge: ……我建议你们这次要调查更彻底一些。

text: Orym: ...
translation: Orym: ……

text: Judge: We will resume the trial tomorrow morning.[p/] Court is adjourned.
translation: Judge: 审理将在明日上午继续进行。[p/]休庭。

text: Ruby: ...Hello, Cuthbert.
translation: Ruby: ……你好，卡斯伯特。

text: Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh, Ms. Tymora.
translation: Tyrion: 啊、[p/][s/]噢，泰摩拉女士。

text: I tensed as she approached me.[p/] I’d been so focused on Celeste’s case,[p/] I hadn’t even thought about how I’d left things with my mentor.
translation: 她走近时，我一下子紧张起来。[p/]我一直专注在塞莱斯特的案件上，[p/]根本就没去想和我导师之间还有没解决的问题。

text: Scaled Lord...[p/] She had probably been in the gallery too...
translation: 鳞甲领主啊……[p/]她刚肯定也在旁听席上……

text: She must've seen how I froze up during the first cross-examination.
translation: 她肯定看到了我在第一次交叉询问时呆若木鸡的丑样。

text: Ruby: ...Your job is to put your client at ease,[p/] not the other way around.
translation: Ruby: ……你的工作是让委托人放心，[p/]而不是反过来啊。

text: Tyrion: Sorry...
translation: Tyrion: 对不起……

text: Ruby: I'm sure she was just snapping you out of it,[p/] but everyone saw her become violent with you.
translation: Ruby: 虽然我肯定她是想让你振作起来，[p/]但每个人都看到她对你作出暴力行为。

text: Ruby: That's not exactly [i]helpful[/i] if you want to prove she's not a violent murderer.
translation: Ruby: 如果要证明她不是施暴的凶手，这并不会[i]有帮助[/i]。

text: Ruby: Make no mistake.[p/] The prosecution will use that to their advantage.
translation: Ruby: 不能再犯错了。[p/]检方会用这一点建立优势。

text: Celeste: ...What’s wrong with you?[p/] He did fine.
translation: Celeste: ……你有什么毛病？[p/]他做得很好。

text: Ruby: McCoy, if the Inquisition didn’t botch the investigation,[p/] Cuthbert would’ve never been able to buy this much time.
translation: Ruby: 麦考伊，如果不是裁判所的调查有疏漏，，[p/]卡斯伯特根本没法争取到这么多时间。

text: Celeste: In case you’ve forgotten,[p/] he’s my defense attorney,[p/] not you.
translation: Celeste: 你别忘了，[p/]我的辩护律师是他，[p/]而不是你。

text: Celeste: I don’t get why he has to take notes from you.
translation: Celeste: 我不明白他为何要在你背后亦步亦趋。

text: Tyrion: ...No, she’s right, Celeste.[p/] I need to do better.
translation: Tyrion: ……不，她说得对，塞莱斯特。[p/]我的确需要做得更好。

text: Tyrion: I can’t ever let myself freeze up like that again.
translation: Tyrion: 我不能再让自己再一次像那样呆在那里。

text: Tyrion: I might not be so lucky next time.
translation: Tyrion: 下一次我大概就没有那么幸运了。

text: Celeste: ...
translation: Celeste: ……

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Ruby: Other than that,[p/] I’ll admit that you did well.
translation: Ruby: 除了那一部分，[p/]我承认你都做得很不错。

text: Ruby: You cross-examined the Commander adequately,[p/] and you handled Steelwind’s rebuttals as well as you could have.
translation: Ruby: 你合理地在交叉询问中指出指挥官的错误，[p/]而且尽你所能地接住了斯蒂尔文德的反驳。

text: I remembered how Prosecutor Steelwind was glaring at me at the start of the trial...
translation: 我回想起斯蒂尔文德检察官在庭审开始时看我的眼神……

text: Even thinking about it was enough to make me nauseous.
translation: 光是想想就让我觉得恶心。

text: "Steelwind"...[p/] Why did that name sound so familiar?
translation: "斯蒂尔文德"……[p/]这个名字为什么那么熟悉？

text: Tyrion: ...Wait,[p/] isn’t House Steelwind one of the Four Pillar Families?
translation: Tyrion: .……等等，[p/]斯蒂尔文德家不就是四大支柱家族之一吗？

text: Celeste: That’s right![p/] Lucio Steelwind is the Grand General of Wyverngarde’s military!
translation: Celeste: 对啊！[p/]卢西奥·斯蒂尔文德就是维文加德王国军的i大将军嘛！

text: Celeste: Hasn't he been caught up in a lot of scandals recently?
translation: Celeste: 他最近不是卷入了好多丑闻吗？

text: Ruby: "Scandals"?[p/][s/] He's been having people killed by the thousands.
translation: Ruby: “丑闻”吗？[p/][s/]他已经让数以千计的人被杀死了。

text: Right...[p/] I remember seeing several articles about him in the Ratholme Gazette.
translation: 是的……[p/]我记得在《瑞思霍姆公报》上看到好几篇关于他的文章。

text: In recent years,[p/] groups voicing their grievances against the Crown had begun to surface in the countryside.
translation: 近年来，[p/] 乡村地区开始出现一些公开表示对王室不满的团体。

text: King Olivier would answer their complaints by unleashing Steelwind’s soldiers on these so-called “insurrectionists”.
translation: 而奥利维尔王的回复就是派遣斯蒂尔文德的士兵镇压这些所谓的“叛乱分子”。

text: More often than not,[p/] their peaceful protests turned violent,[p/] leading to bloody retaliations from Lucio's forces.
translation: 他们的和平抗议往往会变成暴力反抗，[p/]导致卢西奥的军队进行血腥报复。

text: Ruby: And Aria is Lucio Steelwind’s only heir.
translation: Ruby: 而这位阿莉亚是卢西奥·斯蒂尔文德唯一的后继。

text: Ruby: When I saw her name on the docket,[p/] I wrote her off as a noble roleplaying for fun.
translation: Ruby: 当我在案件记录上看到她名字的时候，[p/]我以为她只是个来玩过家家的贵族。

text: Ruby: But she’s good—[p/]better than most of the prosecutors [i]I’ve[/i] faced.
translation: Ruby: 但她做得很好——[p/]比我大多数[i]抗衡过[/i]的检察官都要好。

text: Ruby: She's taking this seriously,[p/] and with what Flinhart was doing,[p/] I can't help but wonder if the prosecutor is acting on her father's behalf.
translation: Ruby: 她非常认真地对待工作，[p/]而且考虑到弗林哈特的活动，[p/] 我不禁怀疑，这位检察官是否是在代表她父亲行事。

text: Celeste: ...
translation: Celeste: ……

text: Tyrion: Do you think Prosecutor Steelwind is protecting the real killer?
translation: Tyrion: 你认为斯蒂尔文德检察官在包庇真凶？

text: Ruby: The Inquisition knights [i]were[/i] the first responders to the scene.
translation: Ruby: 裁判所的骑士们是[i]最先[/i]赶到现场的。

text: Ruby: Didn't that strike you as odd?
translation: Ruby: 你不觉得这很奇怪吗？

text: Tyrion: ...Right.[p/][s/] In a small town like Kornhaven,[p/] the village militia would be the first people there.
translation: Tyrion: ……对啊。[p/][s/]像科尔哈文这种小地方，[p/]驻村卫兵才应该是最先赶到的。

text: Ruby: For the Inquisition to respond so quickly,[p/] they would've needed to know that magic was used at all.
translation: Ruby: 裁判所能这么快响应，[p/]他们应该是知道这个案件使用了法术。

text: Ruby: This whole mess feels a little too staged,[p/] and with a [i]Steelwind[/i] involved,[p/] this whole situation reeks of corruption.
translation: Ruby: 整个过程都感觉像是安排好的，[p/]而且[i]斯蒂尔文德[/i]也参与了进来，[p/] 从里到外都散发着腐败的气息。

text: Ruby: I'm sure locking up Flinhart's daughter would send a clear message to his allies.
translation: Ruby: 我确信，拘捕弗林哈特的女儿会给他的盟友们传递明确的信号。

text: But if that were true,[p/] why did she help us in the dungeons?
translation: 但如果真是这么回事，[p/]她为什么要在地牢里帮我们？

text: Tyrion: ...Either way,[p/] this works out in our favor.
translation: Tyrion: 不管怎样，[p/]现在的情况对我们更好。

text: Tyrion: I can't fight the Steelwinds on a battlefield,[p/] but I can fight them in the courtroom.
translation: Tyrion: 我没法在战场上与斯蒂尔文德家抗衡，[p/]但在法庭里我可以。

text: Ruby: On that subject,[p/] I’ve been meaning to give you this.
translation: Ruby: 说到这个，[p/]我一直想把这个交给你。

text: Celeste: My Spell Compendium?
translation: Celeste: 是我的法术汇编？

text: Ruby: I grabbed it from the dining room before the Inquisition arrived.
translation: Ruby: 在裁判所抵达前，我从酒馆的餐厅拿走了这个。

text: Ruby: They have a bad habit of "misplacing" pieces of evidence like this.
translation: Ruby: 他们可是有把这类证据“搞丢”的坏习惯。

text: Tyrion: So...[p/] [i]this[/i] is your Spell Compendium,[p/] and it only has one spell.
translation: Tyrion: 那么……[p/][i]这个[/i]就是你的法术汇编，[p/]而且确实只有一条法术。

text: Celeste: [b]Mage Blade[/b]...
translation: Celeste: [b]法术剑刃[/b].……

text: Ruby: The prosecution will need to prove that [b]Mage Blade[/b] was used in the murder.
translation: Ruby: 检方需要证明犯罪过程中使用了[b]法术剑刃[/b]。

text: Ruby: It will restrict their plan of attack,[p/] which will give you an edge over them.
translation: Ruby: 这会限制她们的进攻策略，[p/]也将使你更有优势。

text: Tyrion: But I still have no idea who actually did this...
translation: Tyrion: 但我仍然不知道究竟是谁干的……

text: Tyrion: Celeste.[p/] I need to talk to you about what your father was doing.
translation: Tyrion: 塞莱斯特。[p/]我需要就你父亲当时的行动和你谈一谈。

text: Celeste: Oh...
translation: Celeste: 噢……

text: She glanced at the bailiff.[p/] He was looming close,[p/] staring daggers at her.
translation: 她看向法警，[p/]他正悍然走近，[p/]用锐利的阳光盯着她。

text: Celeste: I-[p/][s/]I should go.[p/] It looks like they can't wait to throw me back into that cell.
translation: Celeste: 我、[p/][s/]我该走了。[p/]似乎他们现在就要把我关回牢房。

text: Tyrion: W-[p/][s/]Wait,[p/] I still—
translation: Tyrion: 等、[p/][s/]等一下，[p/]我还得——

text: She turned away and quickly walked off without another word.[p/] Ms. Tymora just shook her head.
translation: 她转身迅速地走开，一言不发。[p/]泰摩拉女士摇了摇头。

text: Ruby: She’s practically begging for a conviction at this rate...
translation: Ruby: 再这样下去，她可是相当于求着被定罪。

text: Bringing up her late father may have struck a nerve.[p/] I would need to be more delicate next time.
translation: 提到她的亡父可能戳中了她的痛处。[p/]下一次得更谨慎地提出来。

text: Tyrion: ...I can speak with her in the dungeons later.
translation: Tyrion: .……我可以稍晚在地牢和她谈一谈。

text: Tyrion: Right now,[p/] I want to take a look at the crime scene.
translation: Tyrion: 现在，[p/]我想看一看犯罪现场。

text: Tyrion: With how negligent the Inquisition were,[p/] I doubt they found everything.
translation: Tyrion: 以裁判所表现的疏忽大意，[p/]我怀疑他们是否发现了所有的线索。

text: Ruby: ...If you’re planning to investigate the crime scene,[p/] you’ll need this.
translation: Ruby: ……如果你计划调查犯罪现场，[p/]那你就需要这个。

text: She reached into her coat and pulled out a small, blue Runestone.[p/] I froze for a moment,[p/] starring wide-eyed at it.
translation: 她将手伸进外套，然后拿出一个小小的蓝色符石。[p/]我愣了一下，[p/]然后瞪大眼睛看着它。

text: Tyrion: I-[p/][s/]Is that—?
translation: Tyrion: 那、[p/][s/]那个不是——？

text: Ruby: It’s an Attorney’s Runestone.[p/] I had it commissioned for you after you passed the exam last month.
translation: Ruby: 是律师符石。[p/]上个月你通过考试后，我就替你定制了这个。

text: Tyrion: ...You’ve had one ready this entire time?
translation: Tyrion: ……你这一路一直备着这个？

text: Ruby: ...
translation: Ruby: ……

text: Ruby: Cuthbert,[p/] are you crying?
translation: Ruby: 卡斯伯特，[p/]你是在哭吗？

text: Tyrion: N-[p/][s/]No...
translation: Tyrion: 没、[p/][s/]没有……

text: I wiped my eyes and delicately stored the Runestone in the pocket of my jacket.
translation: 我擦了擦眼睛，小心翼翼地将符石保存进自己的口袋。

text: Ruby: You embarrassed them today.[p/] Don’t expect them to be that sloppy again.
translation: Ruby: 你今天让他们出尽洋相。[p/]别指望他们明天还会这么散漫。

text: Ruby: As much as I hate to admit it,[p/] your investigative mind is sharper than mine.
translation: Ruby: 虽然我不愿意承认，[p/]你的调查思维比我更敏锐。

text: Ruby: If anyone can get through this,[p/] it’s you.
translation: Ruby: 如果有人能解决这个难题，[p/]那一定是你。

text: Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/] Um...[p/][s/] Thank you!
translation: Tyrion: 噢、[p/][s/]哦……[p/][s/]呃——感谢您！

text: She turned to take a seat at the bench near the courtroom entrance.
translation: 她走向法庭入口的一把长椅，坐了下来。

text: It looked like she would be staying here until the case was over.
translation: 看起来在案件结束前，她都会待在这里。

text: I tucked Celeste’s Spell Compendium into my jacket.
translation: 我把塞莱斯特的法术汇编塞进自己的夹克。

text: I hadn’t slept,[p/] and my body still ached from the carriage ride.
translation: 我一直没睡觉，[p/]身体因为驾马车长途跋涉浑身酸痛。

text: I still didn't know whether I was truly up to this,[p/] but none of that mattered.
translation: 我仍然不知道自己是否真的胜任，[p/]但这已经不重要了。

text: Celeste was counting on me,[p/] and I wouldn't let anyone get away with killing Flinhart—[p/]even if the Steelwinds were involved.
translation: 塞莱斯特现在全指望着我，[p/]而我不会让任何杀害弗林哈特的人逍遥法外——[p/]即便是斯蒂尔文德家参与其中。

text: The Inquisition had set up a teleportation circle at the crime scene.[p/] As the defense attorney for this case,[p/] I was permitted to use it.
translation: 裁判所已经设立了通向犯罪现场的传送门。[p/] 作为本案的辩护律师，[p/]我被允许使用它。

text: With it,[p/] I could instantly travel back and forth between the McCoy Tavern and the capital.
translation: 有了这个，[p/]我可以在麦考伊酒馆和首都间瞬间完成往返。

text: It was miles better than having to travel by carriage.
translation: 那可比坐马车好到不知道哪里去了。

text: Orym: Oh...[p/] It’s you...
translation: Orym: 哦……[p/]是你……

text: Orym: What are you doing here?
translation: Orym: 你来干什么？

text: Commander White stepped in my way as I approached the tavern entrance.
translation: 我一走进酒馆，怀特检察官就走来挡住我的路。

text: This time,[p/] I took out my Attorney’s Runestone and PRESENTED it to him.
translation: 而这一次，[p/]我拿出自己的律师符石，<size=1.5em>出示</size>给他。

text: Tyrion: I’m the defense attorney in this case.[p/] I have every right to look at the crime scene.
translation: Tyrion: 我是本案的辩护律师。[p/]我完全有权利调查犯罪现场。

text: Orym: ...
translation: Orym: ……

text: Orym: Whatever...[p/] Just remember,[p/] I've got my eye on you.
translation: Orym: 随你便……[p/]但给我记住，[p/]我一直盯着你。

text: Orym: ...You can take that rock out of my face now.
translation: Orym: ……你可以把那块石头从我脸上拿开了。

text: Tyrion: S-[p/][s/]Sorry...
translation: Tyrion: 那、[p/][s/]对不起……

text: Player: Bed
Tyrion: It's a fancy-looking bed.
translation: Tyrion: 看起来很豪华的床。

text: Jolene: Do you like it?[p/] Celeste had it custom-made by someone in the city.
Tyrion: ...Wouldn’t something like this be really expensive?
Jolene: It was.[p/] I was really surprised when she returned from her travels.
Jolene: She was making a lot of gold from her job as a mercenary.
Jolene: I’m happy for her,[p/] but I still wish she'd do something less dangerous...
Player: Door
Tyrion: It's a door leading to the upstairs hallway.
Player: Table
Tyrion: It's a table with several chairs.[p/] It’s probably used for entertaining guests.
It didn’t look like it's seen much use, though...
Player: Painting
Tyrion: It's a painting of a woman with a strange animal.[p/] The text says “Madeline Kipling”.
Jolene: Oh,[p/] I’ve always loved that painting![p/] Celeste brought it back from her travels.
Jolene: That girl runs a wildlife sanctuary for endangered species.
Jolene: I think that creature is called a "Feykip".[p/] They live in the swamps of the Eastern Kingdoms.
I never knew that creatures like this even existed.
Back
Flinhart McCoy
Tyrion: Can you tell me more about your husband?[p/] Why did he hate magic so much?
Tyrion: I understand that magic is a symbol of the nobility,[p/] but things sounded different with him.
Tyrion: It’s as if he thought Celeste had betrayed him somehow.
Jolene: ...I wasn’t there when they were arguing,[p/] but I can only imagine what he said to her.
Jolene: He’s never had anything good to say about mages,[p/] and I could see how much his words indirectly hurt her.
Tyrion: Did something happen?
Jolene: ...I don’t really know. [h="I don’t trust him enough.|I shouldn't talk about Effie." e="fs"/]
Who was “Effie”?
“Effie”
Tyrion: Does Flinhart’s resentment have something to do with Effie?
Jolene: ...!
Jolene: ...How do you know about her?[p/][s/] There shouldn't be any records of what happened.
Tyrion: I have my ways of finding out.
Jolene: ...Of course.[p/] You work for Ruby Tymora.
Jolene: What did she do?[p/] Did she strong-arm one of the neighbors into talking?
Jolene: We don’t need people like her going around Kornhaven and opening up old wounds.
Tyrion: What?
Jolene: Flinhart always idolized her,[p/] but I could never stand her methods.
Tyrion: ...Is this about the rumors?
Jolene: ...”Rumors”?
Tyrion: When my mentor started fighting against the justice system,[p/] the nobility started levying accusations of misconduct against her.
Tyrion: They spread rumors that she engaged in bribery,[p/] forgery,[p/] and blackmail—[p/]all to discredit her.
Jolene: ...Is that really true?
Tyrion: She personally taught me how to defend persecuted mages like your daughter.
Tyrion: You’ve seen how I defended Celeste.[p/] I’m doing everything honestly and legally.
Jolene: ...
Tyrion: Ms. McCoy,[p/] I know you don’t know who to trust right now,[p/] but I need to know more about Flinhart.
Tyrion: Right now,[p/] I don’t have any leads as to who might’ve done this.
Jolene: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] ...Okay,[p/] but promise me that you won't mention any of this in court.
Jolene: I don't want Celeste being dragged into another scandal,[p/] and I sure as hell don't want any more Steelwinds showing up in town.
Tyrion: I can't make any promises,[p/] but my priority will always be to keep your daughter safe.
Jolene: I guess that's as much as I can ask for...
Flinhart’s Past
Jolene: Effie was Flinhart’s younger sister.
Jolene: Celeste doesn't know about her.[p/] She died twenty years ago,[p/] and Flinhart never brought her up.
Tyrion: What happened?
Jolene: She was friends with the son of the ruling noble back then,[p/] and he’d always sneak into town to see her. [h="...He was so different back then." e="sa"/]
Jolene: Effie had always loved magic.[p/] It reminded her of the fairy tales she read growing up.
Jolene: He’d always show her his spells by the lake,[p/] all to make her smile.
Jolene: But he’d been warned not to use his spells without his instructor present.
Jolene: ...One day,[p/] he lost control of one of his spells and wounded Effie.
Jolene: We rushed her back into the town,[p/] but it was too late.
Jolene: By the time we could dress her wounds,[p/] she was already gone.
Stories like that were awfully common among the nobility these days...
Jolene: The next day,[p/] the Arcane Inquisition came to investigate what happened.
Jolene: The boy’s parents used their influence to cover everything up.[p/] They even threatened Flinhart’s family into staying quiet.
Jolene: After that,[p/] they told the town that Effie had been mauled to death by a wolf.
Jolene: Flinhart all but declared war on the nobility after that.
Jolene: He turned our tavern into a meeting place for commoners who shared his resentment.
Jolene: Word spread,[p/] and night after night,[p/] more and more revolutionaries started showing up to his rallies.
Jolene: It wasn’t long before they started planning a revolution.
Jolene: They started contacting like-minded groups in the towns nearby.[p/] They were even starting to arm themselves.
Jolene: I could only watch as everything unfolded.[p/] I'd heard stories about what the Steelwinds were doing to people like him.
Jolene: I begged him to stop,[p/] so did some of the other townfolk.[p/] We knew he was going to get all of us killed.
Jolene: ...I was shocked when the knights told me he’d been murdered,[p/] but I wasn’t surprised.
Jolene: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] I really hoped he’d let go of his anger when Celeste showed up.
Jolene: He was really starting to change after we adopted her.
Celeste McCoy
Tyrion: Can you tell me more about Celeste?[p/] How did she become a part of your family?
Jolene: It all happened nineteen years ago,[p/] a year after Effie’s death.
Jolene: We were closing one night when we found a baby with white hair abandoned in the dining area.
Jolene: We waited weeks for her parents to come by,[p/] but no one ever came looking for her.
Jolene: We named her “Celeste”,[p/] after the full moon that was out that night.
Jolene: We’d always wanted a child,[p/] so I thought she must have been a gift from the Scaled Lord.
Jolene: But one day,[p/] I spotted Celeste practicing her magic.
Jolene: I think that’s when I realized that a noble must have abandoned her at our inn.
Jolene: I didn’t care, though.[p/] I loved her all the same.[p/] Flinhart did too.
Tyrion: Wait...[p/] You [i]knew[/i] that she was a mage this entire time?
Jolene: I did.[p/] You haven't known her very long,[p/] but she's a terrible liar.
Jolene: Flinhart must have noticed it too,[p/] but I think that he didn’t want to believe it. [h="It’s my fault.|I should have forced him to see the truth." e="s"/]
Flinhart and Celeste
Tyrion: ...I spoke with Celeste earlier,[p/] and she believes her father died hating her.
Jolene: What?![p/][s/] He loved her more than anything in the world!
Tyrion: They must’ve been really close.
Jolene: More than anything.
Jolene: ...Do you want to hear a story?
Jolene: Celeste had just come from the Eastern Kingdoms.[p/] She wouldn’t stop talking about this foreign drink that she loved.
Jolene: Flinhart did everything he could to have it shipped here.[p/] He even called in a bunch of favors to do it.
Jolene: In the end,[p/] he was only able to get a single bottle of it.
Tyrion: Is it really that rare?
Jolene: It’s a product of the Eastern Kingdoms,[p/] and it’s hard to import anything from outside of Wyverngarde.
Jolene: I wouldn’t be surprised if it were the only bottle of Lotus Juice in this region.
Wait a minute,[p/] “Lotus Juice”?
Tyrion: Is this the bottle that Flinhart bought?
Jolene: Oh no...[p/] He was going to surprise her with it.
Jolene: I wondered where it had disappeared to.
Tyrion: I found it shattered on the floor of the dining area.
Jolene: ...That’s strange.
Jolene: Celeste would always help herself to our pantry,[p/] so he was hiding it in our room.
Tyrion: Really?[p/] When was the last time you saw it?
Jolene: It was right before I left the house that night.
Jolene: It would’ve been in his room before their argument.
Back
Runestone
Jolene: Oh?[p/] What’s this?
Tyrion: It’s an Attorney’s Runestone.[p/] It identifies me as a certified attorney.
Jolene: Wow.[p/] You must have worked very hard to get that.
Tyrion: Yeah,[p/] it also has a magical tool that lets me take pictures of crime scenes.
Jolene: Wow,[p/] that’s amazing!
Something about her overly enthusiastic tone reminded me of my late mother.

Tyrion: It's fine...[p/] It helped me more than anything.

Garrick: Yes.[p/] Now that you mention it,[p/] I do recall that happening.
Garrick: Although, I have no idea why she would do that.
Garrick: Unless you can prove that she didn’t do exactly what I described?
Tyrion: I suppose not...
At least not yet, anyway...
Player: Overflow
Celeste: He keeps saying that he stood at the entrance the entire time.[p/] That has to be a lie.
Tyrion: Right...[p/] If he stabbed Flinhart,[p/] he would’ve been a lot closer to him.
How would I prove that, though?
Player: Objection

text: Tyrion: Your Honor![p/][s/] It's clear that the witness's testimony is contradictory!

text: Tyrion: And this piece of evidence proves it!
translation: Tyrion: 而这件证据证明了这点！

text: Judge: I fail to see how that proves anything, Mr. Cuthbert...
translation: Judge: 卡斯伯特先生……我没看出来这两者有什么关系。

text: Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh...[p/] I guess you're right.
translation: Tyrion: 噢、[p/][s/]哦……[p/]我想您说得对。

text: Tyrion: Pierce,[p/] you said that you illuminated the scene from the tavern entrance?
Tyrion: Did you take a single step inside the building?
Garrick: Of course not.[p/] I remember it very clearly.[p/] I only stood at the entrance.
Tyrion: Then maybe you can help me go over this map of the tavern.
Tyrion: If we take a look at this map,[p/] that would place you right here,[p/] where the "W" is.
Garrick: Of course.[p/] Do I need to repeat myself?
Tyrion: Well, I find it interesting that you illuminated the crime scene from all the way back there.
Tyrion: Considering,[p/] that [b]Conjure Light[/b] only illuminates an area within 15ft of the caster!
Garrick: What?!
Tyrion: Pierce,[p/] [b]Conjure Light[/b] doesn’t behave like a normal light source!
Tyrion: It only provides illumination up to 15ft of the caster!
Tyrion: If you had stayed at the entrance,[p/][s/] your spell wouldn’t have shown you anything!
AriaC: The spell’s range is irrelevant.[p/] We’ve already established that the victim would have been illuminated by [b]Mage Blade[/b].
Tyrion: The witness clearly stated that he couldn’t see what was happening.
Tyrion: [i]That[/i] was why he cast [b]Conjure Light[/b] in the first place.
Judge: But what does this mean?
Judge: Lord Pierce,[p/] did you illuminate the crime scene or not?
Garrick : W-[p/][s/]well,[p/][s/] I—
Tyrion: I can answer that question for you, Your Honor.
Tyrion: The witness [i]did[/i] illuminate the scene of the crime!
Tyrion: He illuminated it because he was standing right where it happened!
Judge: ...Mr. Cuthbert?[p/][s/] W-[p/][s/]What are you getting at?!
Tyrion: Your Honor![p/][s/] The defense would like to formally accuse Garrick Pierce of murdering Flinhart McCoy!
Garrick: Hold on for a moment![p/][s/] This is absurd!
Garrick: I-[p/][s/]I could have easily used a different spell to illuminate the room.
Tyrion: And which spell would that be, Pierce?
Garrick: I-[p/][s/]I...[p/][s/] Uh...
Garrick: The specific spell escapes me.
Garrick: I’m not sure which one I used now that I think about it...
AriaC: ...You idiot.
Tyrion: Well, maybe we can figure it out together.
Tyrion: ...When you relinquish your Spell Compendium to the court!
Garrick: Steelwind![p/][s/] Say something!
AriaC: If you had listened to my instructions,[p/] you wouldn’t be in this situation.
AriaC: You’ve made your bed.[p/][s/] Now lie in it.
Garrick: Steelwind,[p/][s/] you treacherous-!
Judge: That’s enough![p/] It appears that the prosecution has no objections.
Judge: With everything that has occurred,[p/] I’m afraid I must ask Lord Pierce to relinquish his Spell Compendium to the court.
Celeste: We did it![p/][s/] We got his Spell Compendium!
With that,[p/] the case was as good as ours.
AriaC: ...
translation: AriaC: ……

text: ...Or was it?
She didn’t even try to stop me from taking his Spell Compendium.
I doubted she’d do that just to spite Pierce.
Tyrion: Your Honor,[p/] I’ve looked at the spells in Lord Pierce’s Compendium.
Tyrion: [b]Conjure Light[/b] is the only spell that he can cast that would shed a blue light.
Tyrion: It's clear that his testimony and many of his statements were false.
Garrick: ...! [h="This little brat!|Such insolence!" e="af"/]
Tyrion: Lord Pierce could very well be a suspect in the murder of Flinhart McCoy.
Tyrion: I’d argue that this presents enough reasonable doubt to acquit the defendant.
Judge: ...Mr. Cuthbert,[p/] I acknowledge that Lord Pierce’s actions are quite suspect.
Judge: However,[p/] I will not pass a verdict just yet.[p/] There is much more we need to learn.
Celeste: What?[p/][s/] That’s not how it works!
Celeste: We proved that there’s reasonable doubt![p/][s/] That should be enough to acquit me, right?!
Tyrion: ...In a normal case, maybe,[p/] but the situation is different for this trial.
Tyrion: The public won’t accept an unsolved homicide involving magic.
Tyrion: Unless we can unequivocally prove your innocence,[p/] the court will keep drawing things out.
Celeste: What!?[p/] But...[p/][s/] that’s not fair!
Tyrion: That’s just how our system works...
Tyrion: The kingdom has always cared more about easing public tension than delivering justice.
Celeste: Are you serious...?
Judge: The court will be suspending the trial of Celeste McCoy until further notice.
Judge: We have no choice but to give the Inquisition time to investigate Lord Pierce as a possible suspect.
AriaC: A “possible suspect"?[p/] The very idea of that is preposterous, Your Honor.
What else did she have up her sleeve?
AriaC: Let’s revisit the basic facts of this case.
AriaC: Flinhart McCoy was murdered using a blade with a [b]transmutation[/b] spell active on it.
AriaC: Mr. Cuthbert,[p/] why don’t you tell me the spells that Lord Pierce knows?
Tyrion: His spells are: [b]Prestidigitation[/b], [b]Conjure Light[/b], [b]Conjure Water[/b], [b]Frost Wave[/b], and [b]Shape Liquid[/b].
Tyrion: ...
translation: Tyrion: ..……

text: Wait a minute...
AriaC: You’ve realized it too,[p/] haven’t you?
AriaC: The witness doesn’t know any [b]transmutation[/b] spells that can be channeled through a solid object!
Tyrion: That can't be right!
Garrick: ...!
Garrick: Ha![p/][s/] You’re right![p/][s/] You’re absolutely right, Steelwind!
Judge: I see...[p/] If all of that is true,[p/] that would still leave the defendant as the only possible killer.
Celeste: But he was the only other mage there![p/][s/] It has to be him!
Tyrion: But he doesn’t know any [b]transmutation[/b] spells that could be cast on your sword.
Tyrion: We can’t prove that he stabbed him while using magic.
Celeste: Wait...[p/] How did he do it then?
Tyrion: ...!
translation: Tyrion: ……！

text: She was right![p/] How [i]did[/i] he do it?
Since the beginning,[p/] I’d been bothered by the traces of [b]transmutation[/b] magic surrounding Flinhart’s body.
Originally,[p/] I’d thought that the killer had somehow magically altered the sword and the surrounding wooden floor.
But none of Pierce's spells could do something like that.
Those [b]transmutation[/b] traces were scattered all over the place.[p/] They didn’t even fully cover the sword.
So what if he didn’t use a sword?[p/] What if he used something else?
Judge: The defense did cast some doubt on this witness’s testimony.
Judge: Nevertheless,[p/] I must conclude that he could not have possibly committed the murder.
Judge: With that in mind—
Tyrion: Please wait, Your Honor!
Tyrion: We still can’t rule out Pierce as a suspect just yet.
Garrick: What are you on about?[p/][s/] Lady Steelwind said it herself!
Garrick: I don’t know any spells that can be channeled through a blade!
Shape Liquid
Default
No...[p/] Pierce didn't have a spell that could be channeled through Celeste's sword.
Which meant that those traces of [b]transmutation[/b] magic must have come from somewhere else.
Not only that,[p/] they must have come from the spell that Pierce used to stab Flinhart.
Tyrion: ...But you didn’t use a metal blade, did you?
Garrick: ...
translation: Garrick: ……

text: Garrick: ...!
AriaC: What nonsense is this?[p/] The autopsy report clearly states that the wound was created by a blade.
AriaC: If he didn’t channel magic through a blade,[p/] how could he have possibly committed the crime?
Tyrion: Steelwind,[p/] Garrick Pierce didn’t kill the victim using a sword...
Tyrion: He killed him using an object shaped exactly like a sword!
Garrick: ...?! [h="No...|Does he know the truth?!" e="fa"/]
AriaC: What...?
Judge: I’m afraid I don’t quite understand, Mr. Cuthbert.
Tyrion: Your Honor,[p/] I'd like to bring your attention to Pierce’s Spell Compendium.
Tyrion: Garrick Pierce knows a seemingly insignificant spell called [b]Shape Liquid[/b].
Tyrion: With it,[p/] he can control any liquid and mold it into any complex shape he wants.
Tyrion: He can even freeze it.[p/][s/] Locking that shape in place!
Tyrion: If he had enough water,[p/][s/] he could create a sword made [i]purely[/i] out of ice!
AriaC: No...[p/] Is it possible that he...?
Tyrion: What do you have to say for yourself, "Lord" Pierce?
Garrick: ... [h="This stubborn little brat!" e="af"/]
Pierce’s breathing became frantic.[p/] He clearly wasn’t prepared to be backed into a corner like this.
I could feel his frustration,[p/] stress,[p/] and fear emanating through the Eye of Horus.
...The board was set.[p/] I just had to deliver the final blow.
Candle
There were some candles lighting up the hallway.[p/] It looked like they were a part of the old architecture.
Door
Several doors led to guest rooms.
Aside from the rooms that Ms. Tymora and I used,[p/] they didn't look like they'd been opened for quite some time.
End Door
There was a door at the end of the hallway.
I saw light from under the door.[p/] Someone was inside.
I tried to open the door,[p/] but it seemed to be locked.
I knocked on the door,[p/] but received no answer in return.
Back
As I made my way up the stairs,[p/] I overheard Steelwind arguing with someone.
Aria: If you keep showing your face here,[p/] people will start asking questions.
Aria: I have the investigation under control now.[p/] All you have to do is stay quiet until this is over.
Garrick: You may be a Steelwind,[p/] but this is my land.[p/] I will do as I see fit.
As I turned down the hallway,[p/] I bumped into what appeared to be a nobleman.
With a quick motion,[p/] he drew a handkerchief to clean the parts of his clothes that had brushed against me,[p/] his cold gaze hardening into a contemptuous glare.
He gave me the same look he'd probably give to a rotting pile of excrement on the Kornhaven streets.
Tyrion: Oh, sorry.
Garrick: Watch where you’re going, you weed.
Garrick: ...Ah![p/][p/] You're that defense attorney from the trial, aren’t you?
Tyrion: ...And you are?
Garrick: I am Garrick Pierce,[p/] the ruling noble of this land.
Garrick: I suppose I should thank you for playing your role in the current trial. [h="If only you weren’t|such a stubborn nuisance..." e="af" /]
Tyrion: ...I suppose that you also believe my client is guilty?
Garrick: I never said that.
Tyrion: But I'm not wrong, am I?
Garrick: ...It matters not what I believe.[p/] This entire scandal has done nothing but rile up the public.
Garrick: Magic is a gift for the nobility and the nobility alone.[p/] It is a symbol of our cosmic right to rule.
Garrick: Bastards like that girl should know their place.
Garrick: This is what happens when the gift of magic is used by those of impure blood.
Tyrion: ...She's just an innocent woman who got wrapped up in all of this.
Garrick: Ha![p/] I must commend you.[p/] You almost had me fooled.
Tyrion: ...Excuse me?
Garrick: Wait...[p/] Do you actually [i]believe[/i] her little story?
Tyrion: ...What good is a defense attorney if they don't believe in their clients?
Garrick: Scaled Lord...[p/] And here [i]I[/i] took you for another envious commoner,[p/] but you actually [i]believe[/i] that trite.
Garrick: Are you sure you’re Tymora’s brat?
I knew I had to pick my battles,[p/] but I really weighed the legal consequences of punching him square in the face.
Garrick: Let me tell you something,[p/][s/] [i]boy[/i].[p/] Even if that bastard is innocent,[p/] the charges have been laid and made public.
Garrick: Someone must take the fall,[p/] lest the public accuse the justice system of corruption.
Garrick: If the King believes there is any unrest here,[p/] even for a moment,[p/] he will unleash Lucio Steelwind and his soldiers upon this region.
Garrick: I'm sure you're well aware of what tends to follow after.
Aria: ...
Tyrion: That doesn't mean my client should be convicted of a crime she didn't commit.
Garrick: What is one insignificant girl when compared to the stability and wellbeing of the people in this land?
Garrick: You're far too short-sighted.
Aria: That's enough.[p/] Stop antagonizing him, Pierce.
He glanced at the Prosecutor for a moment and then back at me.
For a moment,[p/] I caught a flicker of fear in his eyes.
I suppose that even House Pierce had reason to fear the Steelwinds.
Garrick: Hmph...
Without another word,[p/] he walked down the stairs.
Aria: ...
Aria: I don’t agree with him.
Tyrion: ...What?
translation: Tyrion: ……怎么了？

text: Aria: Our system should exist to deliver truth and justice,[p/] not quell public opinion.
Tyrion: ...If you really believed that,[p/] you wouldn't be working against my client.
Tyrion: Do you honestly think she's the kind of monster who would murder her own father?
Aria: ...I’m not so superficial that I’d judge someone based on their appearance.
Aria: The only thing I trust is evidence,[p/] and all of it points to her as the only possible killer.
Tyrion: ...Is that really what you believe?
Tyrion: Or are you just hiding the fact that you're here on your father's behalf?
Aria: ...
Aria: I am [i]not[/i] working with my father! [h="I'm working against him|just as much as you are!" e="sa"/]
What?[p/] She's working [i]against[/i] her father?
Aria: ...
Aria: My apologies.[p/] I don't know what came over me.
Tyrion: It's fine...
Aria: I respect you, Mr. Cuthbert.
Aria: You should fight according to your own ideals.[p/] Just as I will fight for mine.
I tried to think of a retort,[p/] but said nothing as she walked past me.
